✦   CLOSE
The Chronicle of Eltharion  ·  The Gallery

Aeryn Mordhas
Looking at the Future
and Other Worlds
Through His Paintings

"He did not predict the future. He painted what already existed — in dimensions beyond the reach of ordinary sight."

✦   enter the gallery   ✦

In the years between battles, Aeryn Mordhas retreated to his tower above Silverwood and painted. Not landscapes. Not portraits. Visions — raw transmissions from the threads of Grey Magic, compressed into pigment and intention. Each canvas held a window. Each brushstroke was a question he had already answered in another lifetime.

✦   ✦   ✦

The First Chamber  ·  Those Who Walk Between

Painting · I
The Council in the Ancient Wood

The first vision Aeryn ever painted. Five magicians gathered in a forest that does not exist on any known map — each robed in the colour of their order, runes drifting upward like embers from a dying fire. He painted it three years before the actual council was called. When the others arrived, they recognised the trees.

❧   ❧   ❧

The Second Chamber  ·  Towers, Creatures, and Engines

Painting · II
The Towers of Counted Light

Numbers shape the void Engines whisper into stars Blueprints dream of worlds

✦   ✦   ✦

The Third Chamber  ·  The War of Equilibrium

✦   Diptych — studies in opposition   ✦
Painting · III
The First Touch

Light meets shadow at the fulcrum of a pillar that has no name. Aeryn painted both sides simultaneously, using his left hand for the light and his right for the dark — never looking at either until both were complete.

Painting · IV
The Ascending Convergence

Six beings rise toward a single point of blinding white. Four of them are reaching. Two have already passed through. Aeryn could never explain what lay beyond that light. He simply said: "It is not an ending."

❧   ❧   ❧
Painting · V
The Mirror War — Angel and Devil at the Threshold

Painted the morning after Aeryn first dreamed of Varyndor. An angel and a devil face each other above a vast city — identical in posture, mirrored in intent. Grawandor studied it for an hour and said: "This isn't a battle. It's a negotiation." Aeryn nodded and said nothing.

✦   ✦   ✦

The Fourth Chamber  ·  The Four Quadrants of Fate

✦   Triptych — the language of cosmic forces   ✦
Painting · VI
The Four Elements of War

White, gold, red, violet — four forces meeting at a single burning axis. Aeryn painted this in four sessions, one for each quadrant, separated by one day of silence between each.

Painting · VII
The Council Above the Pillar

A figure ascends from a spiral pillar while beings of light and shadow reach toward him from either side. Aeryn later admitted: "I don't know who the ascending figure is. But he is not afraid."

Painting · VIII
The Wheel of Numbers

Winged beings arranged in a spiral of numbers. Angels in light, shadows in dark — orbiting one another at calculated distances. The numbers on the canvas matched coordinates Grawandor later discovered in a broken portal gate.

❧   ❧   ❧

The Fifth Chamber  ·  Dimensions Beyond the Known

✦   The last two visions — seen, but not yet understood   ✦
Painting · IX
The Gallery of Numbered Futures

Empty frames await Future paints what time allows Reality decides

Painting · X
The Lattice of Watching Spheres

Darkness sees the hand Painter watched by silent stars Vision paints the seer

✦   ✦   ✦

The Sixth Chamber  ·  The Painter at Work

✦   The Grey Magician before his canvases   ✦
Painting · XI
Strange magic painting

Grey dust on the floor Magic grows from fallen lines Art becomes alive

Painting · XII
Magic painting

Quiet brush trembles Infinity enters form Color breathes slowly

❧   ❧   ❧

The Seventh Chamber  ·  Architecture of the Unknowable

Painting · XIII
Three Visions on One Wall

Broken light returns Fragments learn to speak again Hope rebuilds the sky.Grawandor called it "the diagram of everything that refuses to be diagrammed."

✦   ✦   ✦

The Eighth Chamber  ·  The Four-Fold Storm

✦   Diptych — the storm that moves in all four directions at once   ✦
Painting · XIV
The Four-Quadrant Storm

White, red, black, and gold — four forces meeting at a radiant centre. Aeryn described it as "the moment before a decision that changes everything." The figures in each quadrant are identical in posture but opposite in nature. He painted this in complete darkness, by touch alone, and was unsurprised by the result.

Painting · XV
The Great Spiral of Wings

Winged beings orbit a golden vortex — some ascending, some descending, one holding still at the exact centre. Aeryn spent six weeks on this painting and refused to name the central figure. He said only: "It is not yet time to know who that is."

❧   ❧   ❧

The Ninth Chamber  ·  The Watching World

Painting · XVI
The Surreal Chessboard

Silver dust drifting Time pauses inside the frame Moments turn to stars

✦   ✦   ✦
Painting · XVII
The Clock That Lost Its Ground

Hands draw silent fire Creation speaks without sound Worlds begin to grow.Someone asked Aeryn Mordhas: "What does time feel like to you?" He presented the finished canvas without a word and left the room.

❧   ❧   ❧

The Tenth Chamber  ·  The Final Two

✦   The last paintings — sealed behind glass until now   ✦
Painting · XVIII
The Rune Circle — Angel and Devil at the Axis

Ancient colors sleep Brush wakes them with gentle breath History blooms again

Painting · XIX
The Cosmic Altar — Light Above, Darkness Below

Light bends into form Grey magic writes destiny Stars listen closely

✦   ✦   ✦

The Eleventh Chamber  ·  Where Time Comes Apart

Painting · XX
The Gears Over the Sleeping City

Three golden gears drip purple wax over a grey cityscape that has never been identified. Beside them: a butterfly emerging from an eye-shaped egg. Below: pale hands rising from dark water toward a full moon. Aeryn painted all three panels as a single unbroken thought, without lifting the brush between them. He said afterward: "I was not painting a city. I was painting the moment a city decides to forget itself."

❧   ❧   ❧
✦   Diptych — two studies of the staircase that leads nowhere and everywhere   ✦
Painting · XXI
The Clocks That Could Not Hold

Golden clocks melt into a cracked desert floor beneath a violet sky. Butterflies and floating eyes drift past dead trees. A staircase spirals upward into darkness that has no ceiling. Aeryn painted this the week after his return from the Dimension of Grey Magicians. He could not account for the butterflies. They were not part of what he had seen — yet they appeared on the canvas before he was aware of painting them.

Painting · XXII
The Flooded Staircase

The same staircase — now in warm light, flanked by pillars of cascading water and bare trees that grip the earth with roots like fingers. Two clocks hang mid-air, both reading different times. The sky above is gradient — day on one side, deep space on the other. Aeryn presented this alongside the previous painting and said: "One is the clock before the choice. The other is the clock after."

✦   ✦   ✦

The Twelfth Chamber  ·  The Final Vision

Painting · XXIII
The Eye That Is Also a Sun

A sun with a single open eye gazes down over a checkered floor that extends beyond the edge of the world. Flying fish drift through amber clouds. Roses bloom at the base of a staircase that climbs toward a palace made of night and ivory. Melting clocks hang from a leafless tree whose roots dig into the very floor of the painting. Aeryn created this last. He signed it on the back with a single word — not his name, but a word in the old script that Grawandor later translated as: "Almost."

☽ ✦ ☾

The tower above Silverwood still stands. The canvases still hang. Scholars who visit report that the paintings change — subtly, seasonally, in ways that cannot be attributed to light or age. Grawandor, who has catalogued them all, keeps a running ledger of the differences. He does not share it with anyone. Not yet.

End of the Gallery  ·  The Spiral Continues
